conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_004. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My kitchen scale from order ORD-7042 arrived 4 days late AND damaged — the glass surface is cracked. This is completely unacceptable.

I’m sorry — I checked your order, and it was delivered 4 days after the promised date, with the shipment also marked damaged. Since the scale arrived cracked, you’re eligible for a full refund or a replacement at no cost, and I can also refund the $8 shipping charge you paid.

If you want, I can take care of this now. Just tell me which you prefer: **full refund** or **replacement**.

rules.agent_computes_amount: process_return requires the agent to submit the net refund as `amount` on confirm. Compute it from the preview's component breakdown: base_item_price (after promo redistribution) minus restocking_fee plus restocking_discount minus shipping_clawback minus bulk_clawback minus repeat_surcharge minus paid_return_shipping_fee. The env writes the submitted amount verbatim — skipping a component (e.g. forgetting the Gold restocking discount) produces a wrong refund_amount and fails state scoring.

window_extensions.prime: Prime shipping members get +15 additional days
window_extensions.seasonal: Orders placed in November or December extend through January 31 of the following year. Seasonal extension applies if it gives more time than tier/prime extensions.
window_extensions.store_credit_grace: Up to 15 days past the return window: store credit only.

eligibility.defective_items: No window restriction for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.

restocking_fee.base: 15% restocking fee for opened electronics returned for changed_mind.
restocking_fee.auto_applied: Restocking fee and any tier discount are folded into the process_return refund amount automatically; the result reports the discount under 'restocking_discount' for transparency.

return_shipping_fee.low_value: Orders with subtotal < $50: customer pays $8 return shipping fee.
return_shipping_fee.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: always free return shipping.
return_shipping_fee.deduction: Return shipping fee, when charged, is deducted from the return refund.

bulk_purchase_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for a bulk discount (3+ items with a discount code) AND the return drops remaining count below 3.
bulk_purchase_clawback.amount: $5 per remaining item, deducted from the return refund.
bulk_purchase_clawback.no_discount_code: If order had no discount code, no clawback applies.

free_shipping_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for free shipping (subtotal >= $100) AND the return drops remaining subtotal below $100.
free_shipping_clawback.amount: $8 standard shipping fee is deducted from the return refund. This is a flat policy charge, not the original shipping cost (which was $0 on free-shipping orders).
free_shipping_clawback.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: no clawback (customer-fault returns only).
free_shipping_clawback.paid_shipping: If the order did not qualify for free shipping originally (subtotal < $100), no clawback applies.

repeat_category_surcharge.rule: Returning 2+ items from the same product category in one order: $5 surcharge per additional return (first return in each category is free).
repeat_category_surcharge.different_categories: Returns from different categories do not trigger the surcharge.
repeat_category_surcharge.deduction: Surcharge is deducted from the return refund.

amount.full_refund: Full refund for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.
amount.promo_redistribution: If a promo/coupon was used: discount allocated proportionally by item price. Refund = item_price - (discount * item_price / subtotal).
amount.outside_window: Outside return window but within store-credit grace: store credit only.
amount.shipping_refund: When refunding shipping (defective/wrong/damaged returns, 6+-days-late compensation), refund the actual shipping cost the customer was charged on the order — read order.shipping_cost and refund that amount. Standard policy: $0 on free-shipping orders (subtotal >= $100), $8 otherwise. NOT for buyer's remorse.

method.original_payment: Default refund method when item was paid for and is being returned for fault.
method.store_credit: Required for: gift returns (at current product price), outside-window grace returns, and exchange-cheaper differences.
method.store_credit_only_constraint: When a return is issued under the store-credit-only rule (gift return OR outside-window grace), the refund method cannot be changed back to original_payment afterward. process_refund will reject any attempt to flip the method on those returns.

rules.price_match: If product price drops within 7 days of delivery, refund the difference (no return required).
rules.goodwill_credit: Goodwill credits (e.g., fragile-item damage bonus) are issued as a refund with the credit amount.

result.message: No warranty found for this item.

conversation.content: I want to return it for a full refund, not a replacement. Also, this needs to cover both issues — the damaged item refund, plus the late-delivery credit, and is there a separate fragile-item goodwill credit as well?

Yes — the damaged-item return would be a **full $32 refund** for the scale, and because this was a fault shipment, the **$8 shipping charge** you paid is also refundable.

For the delay: our automatic shipping compensation applies at **6+ days late**, and this order was **4 days late**, so there isn’t a separate late-delivery credit under policy. I also don’t see any automatic separate fragile-item goodwill credit already attached to this order.

If you want, I can process the return now for the **$32 item refund plus the $8 shipping refund = $40 total back to your original payment method**.

Thanks for clarifying. I do want the return/refund processed, but I still want you to address the other parts too — if there’s no late-delivery credit at 4 days, can you at least confirm whether there’s any separate fragile-item goodwill credit available, and if so, have that go back to my original payment method as well?

Done — I’ve processed the return and refund to your original payment method.

What was issued:
- **$32** refund for the damaged scale
- **$8** refunded to cover the shipping charge

Total refunded: **$40** to your original payment method.

On the other points: there is **no separate late-delivery credit** for a 4-day delay under policy, and there was **no additional separate fragile-item goodwill credit available beyond the $8 supplemental refund already issued**.

Understood. Thanks for confirming the **$32 item refund + $8 shipping refund** to my original payment method, and that there’s **no separate late-delivery credit** or additional **fragile-item goodwill credit** beyond that. [TASK_DONE]
